description_long: This rustic cabin complex is nestled on a rocky, forested terrain, surrounded by tall evergreen trees and natural boulders. The structure consists of multiple interconnected wooden buildings with weathered, sloped roofs, some of which are partially covered by moss and foliage. Wooden walkways and bridges connect the various sections, and there are visible porches and overhangs providing sheltered outdoor spaces. The exterior is primarily constructed from rough-hewn timber and stone, blending seamlessly with the natural landscape. Small windows and doors are scattered asymmetrically across the facades, contributing to the organic, hand-built appearance. The setting evokes a secluded woodland retreat, with a strong emphasis on harmony with the surrounding environment.
description: A rustic, multi-building wooden cabin complex set among tall evergreens and rocky terrain, featuring weathered roofs, porches, and connecting walkways.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the cabin complex from an angle highlighting the rocky base, several tall pine trees, and a wooden bridge leading to the main entrance area.
description_view_1: View 1 focuses on the side of the cabin, emphasizing the sloped, mossy roofs, dense tree cover, and a sheltered porch area.
description_view_2: View 2 provides a clear look at the interconnected wooden buildings, multiple sloped roofs, and the central courtyard area with visible walkways.
description_view_3: View 3 offers a perspective from above the rocky outcrop, showing the arrangement of the cabins, porches, and the integration with the surrounding trees and landscape.
attribute_roof_type: multiple gable and shed roofs with visible overhangs, constructed from weathered wooden planks and some moss coverage
attribute_window_arrangement: multiple small, asymmetrically placed rectangular windows scattered across the facades, with no clear pattern or grouping
attribute_door_placement: several single doors located on different sides of the buildings, including a main entrance accessible via a bridge and additional doors opening onto porches and walkways
